meta.notes: All 17 protocols approved by MD. All services active.

dose_reduction_protocol.action: Drop one titration step for 4 weeks, then re-attempt escalation. If still intolerable, maintain at tolerated dose.

gallbladder_workflow.action: Flag for provider review. Patients with gallbladder disease or prior cholecystectomy: monitor for biliary symptoms. Rapid weight loss increases cholelithiasis risk.

disqualifiers.reason: GLP-1 slows gastric emptying — absolute CI in gastroparesis
Personal history of pancreatitis — absolute CI
MTC risk — GLP-1 class black box warning (rodent thyroid C-cell tumors)
MEN-2 syndrome — absolute CI per FDA labeling

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks after each dose escalation (async symptom check). Every 3 months at maintenance.

escalation_triggers.trigger: Severe nausea/vomiting persisting >2 weeks at current dose
Signs of pancreatitis (severe abdominal pain radiating to back)
escalation_triggers.action: HOLD. ER referral. Do not resume without provider clearance.
Flag for provider review. Ultrasound if indicated.

ten_mg_gate.action: If losing <0.5 lbs/week for 4 consecutive weeks at 10mg → escalate to 12.5mg. Otherwise maintain 10mg. Provider task required — cannot auto-escalate past 10mg.

dose_reduction_protocol.action: Drop one titration step for 4 weeks, then re-attempt. Same as semaglutide pattern.

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks after each escalation. Weekly weight log at 10mg gate. Every 3 months at maintenance.

escalation_triggers.trigger: At 10mg for 4 weeks with <0.5 lbs/week loss
escalation_triggers.action: Generate provider task to review escalation to 12.5mg

adjustment_rules.action: Start at 25mg instead of 50mg
Max 25mg
Start at 25mg, max 25mg
Increase to 100mg PRN. Provider approval required for dose increase.
Decrease to 25mg PRN

disqualifiers.reason: Absolute CI — severe hypotension risk with nitrates (nitroglycerin, isosorbide)
Hypotension risk — timing separation required. Provider review.

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks: async efficacy + side effects. Every 6 months: refill + check.

prn.timing: 30 minutes before sexual activity (effective up to 36 hours)

adjustment_rules.action: No daily dosing. PRN max 5mg once every 72 hours.
Absolute CI — Child-Pugh C
PRN max 10mg. No daily dosing recommended.
Increase to 20mg PRN
Increase to 5mg daily

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks: async efficacy check. Every 6 months: refill.

escalation_triggers.trigger: Back pain or myalgia (common at higher doses)

titration.label: Dose adjustment based on trough T level

adjustment_rules.action: Increase by 20mg/week. Recheck at 6 weeks.
Decrease by 20mg/week. Recheck at 6 weeks.
HOLD testosterone. Sync visit. Therapeutic phlebotomy may be indicated. Do not resume until Hct <50%.
Provider review. Consider anastrozole if symptomatic gynecomastia. Do not auto-prescribe AI.

disqualifiers.reason: Two low T measurements required for diagnosis (<300 ng/dL each, morning draw, ≥1 week apart)
Active prostate cancer — absolute CI per FDA labeling
Active male breast cancer — absolute CI per FDA labeling
Sync visit + urology referral before prescribing
Untreated severe OSA — provider review. Not absolute CI but monitor.
Exogenous T suppresses spermatogenesis. Counsel on fertility impact. Consider enclomiphene instead.

required_labs.panel: Total testosterone x2 (morning, fasting, ≥1 week apart)

required_labs.frequency: Baseline if age ≥40, then every 6 months

monitoring.schedule: 6 weeks: trough T + CBC. 3 months: trough T + CBC + E2. Every 6 months: full panel (T, CBC, E2, CMP, lipids, PSA if ≥40).

escalation_triggers.trigger: Chest pain, SOB, or neurological symptoms

testosterone-injectable.dea_note: Schedule III — requires synchronous video visit per DEA telehealth waiver (through Dec 31, 2026). One sync visit satisfies requirement.

titration.action: Titrate based on symptoms + E2 level. Increase to 0.075 or 0.1 mg/day if inadequate response.

adjustment_rules.action: Increase patch dose by one step (0.05→0.075→0.1). Recheck E2 at 6 weeks.
MANDATORY: Co-prescribe progesterone (Prometrium). Postmenopause: 100mg/day continuous. Perimenopause: 200mg x12 days/month.
adjustment_rules.system_behavior: Auto-generate progesterone co-prescription task. Cannot issue estrogen prescription alone.

adjustment_rules.action: Counsel on application technique. Consider switch to estrogen-cream-systemic if persistent.

progesterone_co_prescribing_rule.system_behavior: Auto-generate co-prescription task if intact uterus. Block estrogen-only prescription without task acknowledgment.

mammogram_workflow.action: Mammogram acknowledgment required before refill authorized. System blocks refill until patient confirms current mammogram.

disqualifiers.reason: Must rule out endometrial pathology before prescribing
High VTE risk with systemic estrogen

required_labs.frequency: Baseline + 6 weeks if dose change + every 6 months

required_labs.skip_condition: age >45 + >12mo amenorrhea + classic symptoms — provider documents rationale

monitoring.schedule: 6 weeks: async symptoms + E2 if dose adjusted. 3 months: async. 6 months: labs (E2, FSH if needed). Annually: labs + mammogram acknowledgment.

escalation_triggers.trigger: VTE symptoms (leg swelling, chest pain, SOB)
New breast mass or abnormal mammogram

dosing.food_requirement: MUST be taken with food — absorption requires dietary fat
dosing.lab_timing_critical: Draw 3-5 hours after morning dose. NOT trough. Fundamentally different from injectable TRT monitoring.

titration.label: 6-week lab review
titration.if_T_above_900: Decrease to 158mg QD or switch to injectable

adjustment_rules.action: FLAG — provider review. Assess CV risk per black box.
HOLD medication. Sync visit. Address hypertension before resuming.
HOLD. Sync visit. Same rule as injectable.
Reinforce food requirement. Do not increase dose until compliance confirmed.
Switch to testosterone-injectable at 100mg/week SC. Discontinue Jatenzo.

dosing.black_box_warning: HYPERTENSION — can increase BP and MACE risk. Evaluate CV risk before prescribing. Monitor BP.

dosing.progesterone_not_required: At standard vaginal doses (0.5g 2x/week), systemic absorption minimal — no progesterone co-prescribing needed

estrogen-cream-vaginal.routing_rule: Intake symptom question: GSM/vaginal only → here. Systemic symptoms (hot flashes, night sweats) → estrogen-patches or estrogen-cream-systemic.

estrogen-cream-systemic.product_name: Compounded Systemic Estradiol Cream — Topical HRT

dosing.application: Inner upper arm or inner thigh, rotate sites

estrogen-cream-systemic.mammogram_workflow: Same as estrogen-patches — annual acknowledgment required before refill.

dosing.md_consent_note: Off-label use acknowledged. TRIUMPH Phase 3 dose-escalation protocol used as clinical reference. Patient informed that retatrutide is not FDA-approved. Patient counseled on GLP-1 class risks. Signed informed consent on file.

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks after each dose escalation (async symptom check). Every 3 months at maintenance.

disqualifiers.reason: Visual symptoms are hard block — SERM class risk
Enclomiphene works via HPG axis — requires functional testes. Primary hypogonadism → route to TRT.

dosing.md_consent_note: MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived peptide with no FDA approval and limited human clinical trial data. Patient informed of experimental status. Signed investigational/experimental compound consent on file. Compound dispensed by 503B facility.

monitoring.schedule: 2 weeks: async tolerability. 4 weeks: async response + titration decision. Every 3 months thereafter.

dosing.md_consent_note: BPC-157 is a research peptide with no FDA approval. Available human data is limited; most efficacy data is derived from animal models. Long-term safety in humans is unknown. Route selected based on clinical indication documented in chart. Signed consent on file. 503B sourced.

monitoring.schedule: 2 weeks: async tolerability. 4-12 weeks: end-of-cycle assessment. Resume or adjust at provider discretion.

dosing.md_consent_note: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4 synthetic fragment) is not FDA-approved. Dosing protocols are practitioner-consensus-based. Prescriber retained clinical discretion over loading duration. Signed consent on file. 503B pharmacy sourced.

monitoring.schedule: 2 weeks: async tolerability. 4 weeks (end loading): async outcome check. Monthly during maintenance.

dosing.md_consent_note: Sermorelin is a growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) analogue. Previously FDA-approved (Geref) but discontinued commercially; available via 503B compounding. Dosing at bedtime to coincide with physiologic GH pulse. Signed consent on file.

disqualifiers.reason: GH-releasing agents contraindicated with active malignancy

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks: async tolerability and sleep/recovery assessment. 12 weeks: outcome review. Every 6 months: IGF-1 level recommended.

escalation_triggers.trigger: Joint pain, edema, or carpal tunnel symptoms
escalation_triggers.action: Hold. Reduce dose or discontinue. Sync visit.

dosing.md_consent_note: Wolverine blend (BPC-157 + TB-500) is a compounded combination peptide with no FDA approval. Combination data in humans is absent. Blend ratio confirmed with dispensing 503B pharmacy; CoA on file. Signed combination compound consent on file.

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks (end loading): async tolerability + outcome. Monthly during maintenance.

dosing.md_consent_note: GLO blend is a compounded three-component peptide with no FDA approval. Patient screened negative for Wilson's disease. Combination safety data unavailable. Blend confirmed with 503B pharmacy; CoA on file. Signed consent on file.

disqualifiers.reason: GHK-Cu contains copper — absolute CI in Wilson's disease

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks (end loading): async tolerability + outcome. Monthly during maintenance.

dosing.kpv_note: KPV preferred over GLO for GI/gut inflammatory indications
dosing.md_consent_note: KLOW blend is a compounded four-component peptide with no FDA approval. Patient screened negative for Wilson's disease. Medication list reviewed for immunosuppressant use — KPV has immunomodulatory activity. KLOW selected over GLO based on documented GI/gut inflammatory indication. Combination safety data unavailable. Blend confirmed with 503B pharmacy; CoA on file. Signed consent on file.

disqualifiers.reason: GHK-Cu contains copper — absolute CI in Wilson's disease
KPV immune-modulating — flag for provider review, not hard block

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks (end loading): async tolerability + outcome. Monthly during maintenance.

disqualifiers.reason: Finasteride is teratogenic — absolute CI in pregnancy or planned pregnancy
Finasteride alters PSA levels — absolute CI with prostate cancer history

monitoring.schedule: PSA at 6 months if age > 40. Every 6 months: async follow-up.

disqualifiers.reason: Spironolactone is teratogenic — absolute CI in pregnancy or planned pregnancy
Spironolactone is renally cleared — provider review required
Spironolactone is potassium-sparing — absolute CI with hyperkalemia

required_labs.reason: Recommended but not required — spironolactone can elevate potassium

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks: async tolerability. Every 6 months: refill + potassium check recommended.

disqualifiers.reason: History of DVT/PE — provider review required
Undiagnosed vaginal bleeding — absolute CI until evaluated

monitoring.schedule: 4 weeks: async tolerability. Every 6 months: refill + provider check-in.
